items.headline: Rockets’ Thompson puts Payton Jr. on a poster with ferocious and-one slam
items.blurb: Rockets’ Thompson puts Payton Jr. on a poster with ferocious and-one slam — Watch as Houston Rockets guard Amen Thompson puts Golden State Warriors guard Gary Payton Jr. on a poster with a ferocious and-one dunk.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo explains why he is hurt by sitting out
items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo explains why he is hurt by sitting out — Giannis Antetokounmpo opened up about his frustration with the Milwaukee Bucks in a new interview, explaining why he is so bothered that the team wants to sit him out for the rest of the season.

items.blurb: Warriors’ Curry blows past Durant and floats in and-one teardrop — Watch as Golden State Warriors guard Steph Curry blows past former teammate Kevin Durant with a hesitation dribble before floating in a beautiful and-one teardrop layup.

items.blurb: The 'NBA active two-pointers leaders' quiz — How many of the active NBA players with at least 4,000 two-pointers made in their career can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Mavericks' Cooper Flagg joins exclusive club with another monster game
items.blurb: Mavericks' Cooper Flagg joins exclusive club with another monster game — Cooper Flagg is making a pretty big statement in his quest for the NBA's Rookie of the Year Award. After scoring 51 points for the Dallas Mavericks in their loss to the Orlando Magic on Friday night, he followed it up with a 45-point effort in a 134-128 win over the Los Angeles Lakers on Sunday.

items.headline: Luka Doncic To Receive Medical Treatment In Europe For Hamstring Strain
items.blurb: Luka Doncic To Receive Medical Treatment In Europe For Hamstring Strain — Luka Doncic will head to Europe to seek specialized medical treatment in an attempt to expedite his return from a Grade 2 hamstring strain, sources told Shams Charania and Dave McMenamin of ESPN. items.headline: Warriors’ Curry splashes deep three for first bucket in return from injury
items.blurb: Warriors’ Curry splashes deep three for first bucket in return from injury — Watch as Golden State Warriors star guard Steph Curry splashes a deep three to score his first bucket in his return from a knee injury that kept him out for two months.

items.headline: Report: Luka Doncic seeking specialized treatment to expedite return from injury
items.blurb: Report: Luka Doncic seeking specialized treatment to expedite return from injury — Doncic is heading to Europe to seek specialized medical treatment on his Grade 2 left hamstring strain in hopes of expediting his return to play, ESPN’s Shams Charania reported Sunday.

items.headline: Dusty May Commits To Michigan, Won't Leave For North Carolina Job
items.blurb: Dusty May Commits To Michigan, Won't Leave For North Carolina Job — Michigan head coach Dusty May has told university officials he is not seeking other coaching positions, according to ESPN sources. The move effectively ends any possibility of May landing at North Carolina, which had identified him as an early candidate. items.headline: Flagg scores 45 points a Mavs beat James and injury-depleted Lakers
items.blurb: Flagg scores 45 points a Mavs beat James and injury-depleted Lakers — Cooper Flagg scored 45 points and the Dallas Mavericks ended their longest home losing streak in 32 years at 14 games with a 134-128 victory over the depleted Los Angeles Lakers on Sunday night.

items.headline: Blazers Exploring Coaching Market
items.blurb: Blazers Exploring Coaching Market — The Portland Trail Blazers are exploring the coaching market to better gauge the team's options, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line. Tiago Splitter is 40-37 as the team's interim coach, with Chauncy Billups placed on indefinite leave by the NBA due to a federal gambling probe. The Blazers are currently eighth in the Western Conference standings. New owner

items.blurb: Pressure 'Mounting' On Arturas Karnisovas; UNC Remain Interested In Billy Donovan — Pressure is "mounting" on Chicago Bulls executive vice president Arturas Karnisovas, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line. items.headline: Bucks Could Part Ways With Or Reassign Doc Rivers; Taylor Jenkins Emerging As Candidate
items.blurb: Bucks Could Part Ways With Or Reassign Doc Rivers; Taylor Jenkins Emerging As Candidate — The Milwaukee Bucks could re-assign or part ways with head coach Doc Rivers this offseason, according to Marc Stein of The Stein Line. Rivers has a record of 95 wins and 100 losses in just over two seasons with the team. The Bucks hired Rivers in January 2024 after abruptly firing Adrian Griffin as head coach

items.headline: Raptors back in play-in position as Celtics coast to season-series sweep
items.blurb: Raptors back in play-in position as Celtics coast to season-series sweep — The Celtics haven’t needed Jayson Tatum against the Raptors this season. And he was merely a ‘nice-to-have’ as Boston coasted past Toronto on Sunday, writes Michael Grange.

items.headline: Increased Belief Giannis Antetokounmpo Will Be Traded This Summer
items.blurb: Increased Belief Giannis Antetokounmpo Will Be Traded This Summer — Expectations are increasing that the Milwaukee Bucks will trade Giannis Antetokounmpo this summer, according to Marc Stein of The Stein Line. items.headline: Cavaliers’ Mitchell breaks Potter’s ankles with slick move
items.blurb: Cavaliers’ Mitchell breaks Potter’s ankles with slick move — Watch as Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell breaks Indiana Pacers centre Micah Potter’s ankles with a slick step-back move.

items.headline: Stephen Curry returns for Warriors after missing two months with knee injury
items.blurb: Stephen Curry returns for Warriors after missing two months with knee injury — Stephen Curry’s pregame warmup is always a must-see show at Chase Center long before the main event, and the cheers were extra loud for his latest session now that he’s finally healthy and back.

items.headline: Lakers, LeBron James face uncertainty without injured Doncic and Reaves
items.blurb: Lakers, LeBron James face uncertainty without injured Doncic and Reaves — Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves are finished for the rest of the regular season due to injuries, and the Los Angeles Lakers could finish anywhere from third to fifth in the Western Conference playoff race.

items.headline: NBA Roundup: Canada’s Riley scores 30 in Wizards loss
items.blurb: NBA Roundup: Canada’s Riley scores 30 in Wizards loss — Nolan Traore hit five three-pointers and finished with 23 points and seven assists, Jalen Wilson added 19 points and Brooklyn beat Washington.

items.headline: JJ Redick outlines Lakers' plan amid Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves injuries
items.blurb: JJ Redick outlines Lakers' plan amid Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves injuries — The Los Angeles Lakers took another major hit on Saturday when the team announced guard Austin Reaves will miss the rest of the regular season with a Grade 2 oblique strain.

items.headline: UCLA Beats South Carolina 79-51 For First Women's NCAA Title
items.blurb: UCLA Beats South Carolina 79-51 For First Women's NCAA Title — UCLA won its first NCAA women's basketball championship Sunday, routing South Carolina 79-51 in the national title game in Phoenix. The 28-point margin ranks as the third largest in Division I women's championship history. The Bruins never trailed, building a double-digit lead after the first quarter and extending their winning streak

items.headline: How Raptors’ weak fourth quarter resulted in loss to Celtics
items.blurb: How Raptors’ weak fourth quarter resulted in loss to Celtics — Brad Fay, Sherman Hamilton, and Matt Bonner break down the Toronto Raptors’ loss to the Boston Celtics, explain why a weak fourth quarter led to the defeat, and detail the dominant play from Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum that made the difference.

items.blurb: Nate Oats Signs New Contract Through 2031-32, Will Be Top 5 Highest-Paid Coach — Nate Oats signed a new contract with Alabama that will make him one of the five highest-paid coaches in college basketball. items.blurb: Al Horford, Quinten Post To Be Re-Evaluated Next Week — The Golden State Warriors will re-evaluate Al Horford next week, the team announced on Saturday. Horford has been out with a strained right soleus. Horford is averaging 8.3 points and 5.0 rebounds on the season. Quinten Post will also be re-evaluated next week with right foot soreness. items.blurb: Five-Star PG Brandon McCoy Jr. Commits To Michigan — Five-star point guard Brandon McCoy Jr. announced his commitment to Michigan on Saturday night. items.headline: Charles Bassey To Sign With Warriors
items.blurb: Charles Bassey To Sign With Warriors — The Golden State Warriors are set to sign Charles Bassey, sources told Michael Scotto of HoopsHype. Bassey averaged 2.8 points and 1.2 rebounds in five games with the Celtics this season. Bassey replaces Omer Yurtseven on the roster, with Yurtseven having completed his second 10-day contract with the team.

items.headline: Neemias Queta: Scores 18 points with three swats
items.blurb: Neemias Queta: Scores 18 points with three swats — Queta notched 18 points (9-10 FG), seven rebounds, four assists and three blocks in 34 minutes during Sunday's 115-101 victory over Toronto. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Jaylen Brown: Scores game-high 26 points in win — Brown posted 26 points (11-20 FG, 1-3 3Pt, 3-5 FT), six rebounds, three assists and two steals in 35 minutes during Sunday's 115-101 win over the Raptors. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Ja'Kobe Walter: Scores 16 points with full line — Walter finished Sunday's 115-101 loss to Boston with 16 points (6-9 FG, 4-5 3Pt, 0-3 FT), two rebounds, one assist, one block and two steals in 32 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Stephen Curry: Coming off bench Sunday night — Curry (knee) will come off the bench for Sunday's game against Houston, Brett Siegel of ClutchPoints.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Michigan Defeats Arizona 91-73, Will Face UConn In National Title Game
items.blurb: Michigan Defeats Arizona 91-73, Will Face UConn In National Title Game — Michigan defeated Arizona 91-73 in the Final Four and will meet UConn in the men's national title game on Monday. The 18-point win is tied for the largest margin of victory between No. 1 seeds since seeding began in 1979. Aday Mara lead the team with 26 points and nine rebounds while shooting 11-of-16 from the field. Big Ten

items.headline: Nuggets' Nikola Jokic joins elite company with performance vs. Spurs
items.blurb: Nuggets' Nikola Jokic joins elite company with performance vs. Spurs — Before Victor Wembanyama took the NBA by storm, the league was marveling over the dominance of Nikola Jokic. And on Saturday night, the veteran legend made history while getting the better of the younger star.

items.headline: UConn Advances To Third National Title Game In Four Years
items.blurb: UConn Advances To Third National Title Game In Four Years — UConn defeated Illinois 71-62 in the Final Four on Saturday night to make their third national title game in four years. Tarris Reed Jr. lead the Huskies with 17 points. Braylon Mullins added 15 points, going 4-7 from three. items.headline: Lauren Betts, UCLA Defense Beat Texas In Low-Scoring Final Four Semifinal
items.blurb: Lauren Betts, UCLA Defense Beat Texas In Low-Scoring Final Four Semifinal — UCLA ended Texas's tournament run Friday in a grinding 51-44 semifinal victory, securing the program's first-ever NCAA championship game berth. The Bruins will face South Carolina on Sunday. Lauren Betts delivered 16 points and 11 rebounds to pace UCLA. The win avenged the Bruins' only defeat of the season, a 76-65 loss to

items.blurb: Joel Embiid Sits Sixers' Loss To Pistons — Joel Embiid missed the Philadelphia 76ers' 116-93 loss to the Detroit Pistons on Saturday due to "right oblique; injury management; [and] illness." Embiid expressed his frustration over getting ruled out of Wednesday's game against the Wizards on Friday. Embiid has not played in both back-to-back games this season.
